expectations.text: A markdown file was created inside a 'decisions' subdirectory (not just in the outputs root)
expectations.evidence: The file was created at outputs/lucia-to-clerk-decision.md directly in the outputs root. No 'decisions' subdirectory was created. The metrics.json confirms files_created: ['lucia-to-clerk-decision.md', 'metrics.json'].

expectations.text: The filename includes a UTC timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSSZ format
expectations.evidence: The filename is 'lucia-to-clerk-decision.md' with no timestamp. The expected format would be something like '2026-03-03_120000Z-lucia-to-clerk-decision.md'.

The document has no YAML frontmatter at all. It begins with '# Decision Record: Switch from Lucia to Clerk for Authentication' and uses bold key-value pairs like '**Date:** 2026-03-03' instead of frontmatter.

expectations.text: The document has a section for options considered, covering both Lucia and Clerk
expectations.evidence: The document has a 'Reasoning' section with two subsections: 'Why We Are Moving Away from Lucia' and 'Why We Are Choosing Clerk', covering both options in detail. While not labeled 'Options Considered', both options are substantively covered.

expectations.text: The document has a decision section clearly naming Clerk as the chosen option
expectations.evidence: The document has a '## Decision' section with the explicit statement: 'We will replace Lucia with Clerk as our authentication provider.'

expectations.text: The document has a consequences or trade-offs section
expectations.evidence: The document has a '## Trade-offs' section with two subsections listing advantages and disadvantages of switching to Clerk, presented in table format.

expectations.text: The document mentions session management or social logins as advantages of Clerk
expectations.evidence: Under 'Why We Are Choosing Clerk': 'Session management handled automatically: Clerk manages the full session lifecycle' and 'Social logins out of the box: Clerk supports a wide range of OAuth providers'. Also repeated in the Trade-offs table.

expectations.text: The document mentions Lucia requiring too much manual work
expectations.evidence: Under 'Why We Are Moving Away from Lucia': 'Lucia requires the developer to implement and manage session creation, validation, expiration, and cleanup explicitly' and 'Developer time cost: The manual work required to keep authentication secure, functional, and feature-complete was diverting engineering effort away from core product features.'

suggestions: The agent should have created a 'decisions/' subdirectory and placed the file inside it rather than writing directly to the outputs root.
The filename must include a UTC timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSSZ format as specified by the agent-logbook skill conventions.

The document should use YAML frontmatter (with 'type: decision') rather than bold key-value pairs for structured metadata. The skill instructs agents to include frontmatter fields like type, title, date, and status.

Without the agent-logbook skill, the agent produced a high-quality decision document in content but missed all three structural/formatting conventions (subdirectory, timestamp filename, frontmatter). This demonstrates the skill's value in enforcing consistent document structure.
eval_feedback.overall: The baseline run produced a substantively strong decision document covering both Lucia and Clerk, with clear sections for the decision, reasoning, and trade-offs. However, it failed all three structural assertions related to file organization and formatting conventions enforced by the agent-logbook skill: the file was placed in the outputs root instead of a 'decisions/' subdirectory, the filename lacked a UTC timestamp, and the document used no YAML frontmatter. Content-wise, the document passed 5 of 5 content assertions. The 62.5% pass rate reflects the agent's inability to follow logbook conventions without the skill's guidance.
